title: Colophon: the Pākṣika Pratikramaṇa is complete
summary: This closing line is the colophon (concluding declaration) of the fortnightly rite. It announces that the Pākṣika pratikramaṇa, as performed by a lay follower (śrāvaka) of the Vidhipakṣa (Anchalgachchha) tradition, has now been fully and properly completed.

segments.plainMeaning: And so the fortnightly pratikramaṇa of the Vidhipakṣa-gaccha lay devotee is finished.
segments.idiomaticTranslation: Thus is completed the Pākṣika (fortnightly) Pratikramaṇa of the śrāvaka (lay follower) of the illustrious Vidhipakṣa Gaccha.
segments.explanation: A colophon, not a chanted prayer: it formally seals the rite, confirming the whole fortnightly observance has been carried out in full. The double daṇḍa (||) marks it as a closing benedictory line.

ritualTerms.gloss: the fortnightly pratikramaṇa, done on the chaudas (14th)
the rite of 'turning back' — reflection, confession, and atonement
the tradition's self-name; the Anchalgachchha (Achhalgach) branch

notes: The native line is Sanskrit in Gujarati script; rendered into IAST. 'iti ... saṁpūrṇam' is a standard concluding colophon formula and is treated as a procedural closing declaration, not a chant. 'śrīvidhipakṣagacchīyaśrāvakasya' is read as a compound: 'of the śrāvaka belonging to the illustrious Vidhipakṣa-gaccha', consistent with the KB's identification of Vidhipakṣa Gaccha = Anchalgachchha. No content invented beyond the native text.
